message:
frankenjax-dynsl1 vectorize batch dynamic_slice with static starts

Adds a batch_dynamic_slice_static_starts fast path that handles the
common vmap case: a batched operand with unbatched (static i64
scalar) start indices. Instead of falling through to the per-element
batch_passthrough_leading rule — which slices each element of the
batch axis individually — the new path:

* Moves the batched dimension to position 0 (move_batch_dim_to_front).
* Prepends a 0 start index for that new leading dim.
* Prepends batch_size to the slice_sizes so the underlying
  DynamicSlice extracts the full batch in a single op.
* Re-issues eval_primitive with the rewritten params; the result is
  re-tagged with batch_dim=0.

Falls through to the existing passthrough rule when:

* No operand is supplied (defensive guard).
* The operand has no batch_dim.
* Any non-operand input is itself batched (mixed-batched starts
  must use the per-element fallback).
* Operand is not a Tensor or its rank doesn't match
  slice_sizes.len() + 1 after the leading-dim insertion.

Three new test cases:

* test_batch_trace_dynamic_slice_batched_operand_static_start_direct_1d
  — 3x5 batched matrix, single static start, slice_sizes=3.
* test_batch_trace_dynamic_slice_batched_operand_static_start_direct_2d
  — 2x3x4 batched tensor, two static starts, slice_sizes=2,2.
* test_batch_trace_dynamic_slice_static_start_moves_nonleading_batch
  — 3x2x4 with batch_dim=1 to prove the move_batch_dim_to_front
  step rotates the batch axis correctly before the fused slice.
